page: UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the STARTER family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 40 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU resources are available on this server type.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression fall into the average tier. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the weak category, although memory bandwidth remains average.

Given the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that require high single-threaded burst performance but do not continuously saturate all cores. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and small-scale text processing or minor LLM inference tasks. The resource density makes it a cost-efficient entry point for development environments, static web hosting, and non-critical background services.
description: UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 40 GB local SSD. The server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though its multi-core performance and database operations align with average benchmarks. Memory performance represents a relative tradeoff, scoring in the lower tier. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this budget-friendly instance is optimized for workloads with moderate resource demands. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight database tasks, development environments, and small-scale text processing applications where high single-threaded burst capability is beneficial without requiring dedicated, continuous multi-core compute resources.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 40 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for entry-level workloads and single-threaded tasks.

8.0 GB system memory and 40 GB local SSD storage
